response.justification: Bogdan has a broad engineering background spanning ~20 years and genuine AI-adjacent work in his current role (MCP code generators, agentic workflows), but the profile falls materially short of a director-level Principal Engineer for AI Strategy. The role demands recognized AI expertise, cloud platform strategy experience, and enterprise customer advisory capability; the resume shows a hands-on, product-focused developer who has not operated at that strategic altitude. No advanced degree, no cloud platform leadership, and no evidence of advising strategic customers or shaping org-wide AI direction make this a poor fit at the seniority level required.
strengths: ~20 years of polyglot engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure, meeting and exceeding the 15-year minimum
Entrepreneurial track record as founder of GISCollective and demonstrable knowledge-sharing through workshops and conference talks (FOSS4G, TDT)
Current hands-on work with AI-agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks at re:cinq shows emerging, real-world AI builder experience
concerns: Seniority ceiling: consistently titled Senior/Staff Software Developer with no evidence of director-level IC scope, cross-org strategy ownership, or executive stakeholder advisory
AI expertise is narrow and recent — limited to agentic tooling in the current role — with no demonstrated AI products at scale, published research, or recognized industry standing in AI
No cloud platform strategy or enterprise cloud expertise; work history is dominated by product engineering and team-level contributions rather than platform or architecture leadership
